headline: Micron surges 15% on memory crunch revenue boom
detail: Micron's revenue quadrupling signals a genuine semiconductor upcycle driven by AI memory demand. This is directly bullish for your chip exposure — AVGO, SMH, and CRWD all benefit from the same AI infrastructure spending wave. Watch whether it gives AVGO enough lift to stabilize before you trim.

headline: Qualcomm stock soars on strong earnings
detail: Another chip name beating hard reinforces that semiconductor fundamentals are strong right now, not broken. This supports holding SMH (your only green chip position, up 11%) and gives some hope for AVGO recovery. Still doesn't change the scanner flags — trim discipline matters on margin.

headline: White House seeks $87.6B emergency spending for Iran war
detail: Large supplemental defense spending adds fiscal pressure and keeps geopolitical risk elevated. Macro uncertainty like this typically pressures high-multiple tech names like PLTR — exactly the wrong environment for a name already down 13% that the scanner says sell.

headline: JPMorgan $50B buyback, Goldman raises dividend post stress test
detail: Strong bank capital returns signal the financial system is healthy, which generally supports broad risk appetite. Positive for SPY and QQQ stability, helping your hedges hold near current levels rather than dragging on your margin cushion.
